**Lucchese 1905 s.r.l.**, or simply **Lucchese**, is an [Italian football](/wiki/Football_in_Italy "Football in Italy") club, based in [Lucca](/wiki/Lucca "Lucca"), [Tuscany](/wiki/Tuscany "Tuscany"). The club was first founded in 1905, having last been in [Serie A](/wiki/Serie_A "Serie A") in 1952.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Foundation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Foundation")]

The club was founded on 25 May 1905, giving the town of [Lucca](/wiki/Lucca "Lucca") its first football team. Originally named **Lucca Football Club** the club was founded by the Vittorio brothers and Guido Mensini. The first large achievement by the club was winning the *Goblet of the King* during the 1919–20 season, not long after the tournament's foundation. The same season Lucca won the Regional cup of Tuscany.

### From U.S. Lucchese-Libertas to A.S. Lucchese Libertas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: From U.S. Lucchese-Libertas to A.S. Lucchese Libertas")]

#### U.S. Lucchese-Libertas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: U.S. Lucchese-Libertas")]

In 1924 the club merged with another local team and changed its name to **Unione Sportiva Lucchese-Libertas**. During the 1920s, notable players at the club included [Ernesto Bonino](/wiki/Ernesto_Bonino_(footballer) "Ernesto Bonino (footballer)") and [Giovanni Moscardini](/wiki/Giovanni_Moscardini "Giovanni Moscardini").

The 1930s saw the club promoted to [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B"), and then in 1936, Lucchese won promotion to [Serie A](/wiki/Serie_A "Serie A"), the top league in [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), where they stayed for three seasons. The club were relegated back to Serie B as World War II started. Prior to the war, players such as [Egri Erbstein](/wiki/Egri_Erbstein "Egri Erbstein"), [Aldo Olivieri](/wiki/Aldo_Olivieri "Aldo Olivieri") and [Antonio Perduca](/wiki/Antonio_Perduca "Antonio Perduca") were fan's favorites. Lucchese won promotion again to Serie A in 1947 and remained there for 5 seasons.

#### A.S. Lucchese Libertas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: A.S. Lucchese Libertas")]

In 1984 the club changed its name to **A.S. Lucchese Libertas**.

The club spent also several seasons in Serie B (last 1998–99) and [Serie C1](/wiki/Serie_C1 "Serie C1"), being also coached by [Luigi Simoni](/wiki/Luigi_Simoni "Luigi Simoni"), UEFA Cup-winning coach with [Inter Milan](/wiki/Internazionale_Milano_F.C. "Internazionale Milano F.C.").

In 2008 the club folded due to serious financial issues and was admitted in the Italian bottom (amateur) division [Terza Categoria](/wiki/Terza_Categoria "Terza Categoria") before being declared formally bankrupt in December 2008.

### From S.S.D. Sporting Lucchese to A.S. Lucchese Libertas 1905

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: From S.S.D. Sporting Lucchese to A.S. Lucchese Libertas 1905")]

#### S.S.D. Sporting Lucchese

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: S.S.D. Sporting Lucchese")]

A new re-born team called **Società Sportiva Dilettantistica Sporting Lucchese** was admitted to [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") to represent the city of [Lucca](/wiki/Lucca "Lucca").

In its first season of existence, Sporting Lucchese promptly won the *Girone E* round of Serie D, thus acquiring the right to take part to the [2009–10 Lega Pro Seconda Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione_2009%E2%80%9310 "Lega Pro Seconda Divisione 2009–10").

#### A.S. Lucchese Libertas 1905

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: A.S. Lucchese Libertas 1905")]

After the promotion, the new club took the denomination of **Associazione Sportiva Lucchese Libertas 1905** starting with the new season, after the Sporting Lucchese owner acquired the naming and logo rights of the old, original team in a public auction. Lucchese made a second consecutive promotion after finishing as champions of Group B of Lega Pro Seconda Divisione. The club has spent the [2010–11 season](/wiki/2010%E2%80%9311_Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "2010–11 Lega Pro Prima Divisione") in [Lega Pro Prima Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione").

However, in the summer of 2011, Lucchese was excluded from professional football by COVISOC for financial irregularity reasons, and didn't appeal against the decision.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

### From A.S.D. F.C. Lucca 2011 to A.S. Lucchese Libertas 1905

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: From A.S.D. F.C. Lucca 2011 to A.S. Lucchese Libertas 1905")]

* **F.C. Lucca 2011**

On 1 August 2011, a new club was founded to represent the city of Lucca: the team was called Associazione Sportiva Dilettantistica Football Club Lucca 2011 and in it was promoted from [Eccellenza Tuscany](/wiki/Eccellenza_Tuscany "Eccellenza Tuscany")[[2]](#cite_note-2) to [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") at the end of the [2011–12 season](/wiki/Eccellenza "Eccellenza").

* **F.C. Lucchese 1905**

The club changed its name to F.C. Lucchese 1905 soon after being promoted.[[3]](#cite_note-lucchese1905-3)

* **A.S. Lucchese Libertas 1905**

In the summer 2013 the club changed his name back to A.S. Lucchese Libertas 1905. On that same season, Lucchese returned to professionalism after winning the Group D of the [2013–14 Serie D](/wiki/2013%E2%80%9314_Serie_D "2013–14 Serie D"), and thus ensuring a spot in the inaugural season of the unified [2014–15 Lega Pro](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Lega_Pro "2014–15 Lega Pro") league.

After a struggling season in the [2018–19 Serie C](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_Serie_C "2018–19 Serie C") that was hit by more financial issues involving the club, Lucchese failed to submit its application for the [2019–20 Serie C](/wiki/2019%E2%80%9320_Serie_C "2019–20 Serie C"), and was declared bankrupt once again on 1 July 2019.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

* **S.S.D. Lucchese 1905**

Immediately after being excluded from Serie C, a further new incarnation of the club, named as S.S.D. Lucchese 1905, was admitted by the Football Federation to [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D").

At the end of the 2024–25 season, Lucchese was excluded from [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C") due to financial difficulties.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Colors and badge

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Colors and badge")]

The team's traditional colors are red and black. The team is nicknamed *Rossoneri*.

## Stadium

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Stadium")]

Their home ground is the [Stadio Porta Elisa](/wiki/Stadio_Porta_Elisa "Stadio Porta Elisa"), which is located on Via dello Stadio in Lucca.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lucchese_1905&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Honours")]

* **[Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B")**
  + Champions: [1935–36](/wiki/1935%E2%80%9336_Serie_B "1935–36 Serie B"), [1946–47](/wiki/1946%E2%80%9347_Serie_B "1946–47 Serie B") (Group B)
* **[Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C")**
  + Champions: 1960–61
  + Runners-up: 1945–46, 1977–78
* **[Serie C1](/wiki/Serie_C1 "Serie C1")**
  + Runners-up: 1989–90
* **[Serie C2](/wiki/Serie_C2 "Serie C2")**
  + Champions: 1985–86, 2009–10
* **[Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D")**
  + Champions: 1968–69, 2008–09, 2013–14
* **[Tuscany](/wiki/Tuscany "Tuscany") League Division 1**
  + Champions: 1929–30, 1932–33, 1933–34
* **[Promozione](/wiki/Promozione "Promozione")**
  + Champions: 1919–20
* **[Coppa Italia Serie C](/wiki/Coppa_Italia_Serie_C "Coppa Italia Serie C")**
  + Champions: 1989–90
